title: Spam and Phishing
drDigitalIntro: Spam is unwanted email — advertisements, fake offers, or worse. Mark emails as spam and they go to your Spam folder. Phishing is a scam where criminals pretend to be your bank or Apple and ask you to click a link or enter your password. Never click links or download attachments from suspicious emails. Never reply with passwords or personal information. If an email looks wrong, ask someone you trust or contact the company directly.

playgroundTask.instructions: One of these emails is a phishing scam. Which one?

items.label: From: Apple
items.preview: Your receipt for iCloud+ ($2.99/month). View in your account at apple.com.

items.label: From: Apple Support ⚠️
items.preview: URGENT: Your Apple ID has been locked! Verify NOW at apple-id-verify.net/login or lose access!

items.label: From: Amazon
items.preview: Your order of 'Running Shoes' has shipped! Track your package: amazon.com/orders

playgroundTask.fakeExplanation: Real Apple emails never link to suspicious domains like 'apple-id-verify.net'. Always check the actual email address and URL, not just the display name.

drDigitalSuccess: You spotted the phishing email! Urgency + suspicious link + strange domain = scam every time.
drDigitalHint: Hover over the link (don't click it!) and check the real URL. Legitimate companies use their own domain, not lookalike URLs.
